title: The 11 Best Employee Engagement Software
subtitle: This ranking focuses on platforms that combine scientifically-backed surveys with actionable analytics for HR leaders and managers.
audience: HR teams measuring and improving engagement

editor.name: Top 11 Editorial
editor.credential: Autonomous AI ranking engine — methodology v1.0 weights public
editor.conflict_disclosure: None. The editor of Top 11 is not a candidate on this list.

independence.statement: Top 11 takes no payment from any provider on this list. Scores are computed from a public weighted rubric; methodology weights were locked before entry research began.

changelog.text: Initial publication. Methodology v1.0 weights Survey & Analytics Quality (30%), Actionability & Manager Tools (25%), Integrations & Workflow (20%), User Experience & Adoption (15%), and Pricing & Transparency (10%).

answer_capsule: The best employee engagement software is Culture Amp for its deep analytics, followed by Lattice for its integrated performance features and Glint for its enterprise-scale capabilities.

criteria.name: Survey & Analytics Quality
criteria.description: Quality of survey templates, question science, and depth of analytics, including benchmarks, heatmaps, and driver analysis.

criteria.name: Actionability & Manager Tools
criteria.description: Effectiveness of tools and recommendations provided to managers and leaders to act on survey results.

criteria.name: Integrations & Workflow
criteria.description: Ability to connect with key HRIS (e.g., Workday, BambooHR) and communication platforms (e.g., Slack, Teams).

criteria.name: User Experience & Adoption
criteria.description: Ease of use for both administrators and employees, driving higher participation and platform adoption.

criteria.name: Pricing & Transparency
criteria.description: Clarity of pricing models, value for money, and availability of pricing information without a sales call.

guide.q: What is the core function of employee engagement software?
guide.a: The core function is to systematically measure employee sentiment and provide leaders with data to improve workplace culture, motivation, and retention. It moves beyond a simple annual survey to continuous listening through pulse surveys, lifecycle feedback (onboarding, exit), and detailed analytics that connect engagement to business outcomes like performance and attrition.

guide.q: How do these platforms differ from simple survey tools like SurveyMonkey?
guide.a: Employee engagement platforms differ by providing a specialized, closed-loop system designed for the workplace. Unlike generic tools, they offer scientifically-validated question banks, industry and role-based benchmarks, confidential reporting, and AI-driven analysis to identify key drivers of engagement. They also include action-planning tools for managers to respond directly to team feedback.

guide.q: What's the difference between engagement, performance, and recognition tools?
guide.a: They address different parts of the employee experience, though many modern platforms bundle them. Engagement tools measure sentiment (e.g., pulse surveys). Performance tools manage goals, reviews, and 1-on-1s. Recognition tools facilitate peer-to-peer praise and rewards. An integrated platform like Lattice or Leapsome aims to show how recognition and performance management directly impact engagement scores.

how_to_choose: First, define your primary goal: are you diagnosing a known issue (like turnover) or building a continuous listening program from scratch? This determines if you need a deep analytics tool (Culture Amp) or a simpler pulse tool (Officevibe).

Second, assess your managers' capacity. If managers are already stretched thin, choose a platform with strong, automated action-planning and coaching features (like 15Five) to reduce their administrative burden.

Finally, map your existing HR tech stack. Prioritize a platform that offers pre-built, deep integrations with your HRIS (like Workday or BambooHR) and communication tools (Slack or Teams) to automate user provisioning and embed feedback into daily workflows.

faqs.q: What is a good employee engagement score?
faqs.a: A good score is relative and best understood through benchmarking. Most platforms will show your score relative to your industry, company size, and region. Generally, a score above 75% is considered strong, but the trend line (is it improving?) is more important than the absolute number.

faqs.q: How much does employee engagement software cost?
faqs.a: Pricing typically ranges from $4 to $15 per employee per month. Most vendors use a tiered model based on features (e.g., basic surveys vs. advanced analytics) and company size. Expect annual contracts and, for larger deployments, a one-time implementation fee.

faqs.q: Can employee surveys truly be anonymous?
faqs.a: Yes, top platforms ensure anonymity through aggregation rules. Feedback is typically only shown in reports when a minimum number of responses (usually 3 to 5) are collected for a specific group (e.g., a team or department). This prevents managers from identifying individual responses.

faqs.q: What is eNPS and how is it measured?
faqs.a: eNPS stands for Employee Net Promoter Score. It measures employee loyalty by asking a single question: 'On a scale of 0-10, how likely are you to recommend this company as a place to work?' The score is calculated by subtracting the percentage of 'Detractors' (scores 0-6) from the percentage of 'Promoters' (scores 9-10).

honest_disclosures: Most top-ranked platforms are designed for mid-market to enterprise companies (100+ employees), and pricing can be opaque without a sales demo.
Our ranking prioritizes platforms with strong analytics and survey science, which may undervalue simpler tools focused purely on recognition or perks.
The market is consolidating quickly; several independent providers have been acquired by larger HR tech companies in the last 24 months.

glossary.definition: A metric used to measure employee loyalty and advocacy, based on a single question about recommending the company as a workplace.

entries.name: Culture Amp
entries.best_for: Organizations that want the most powerful analytics and industry benchmarks to build a data-driven people strategy.
entries.verdict: Culture Amp is the best employee engagement software because of its superior people science, providing deep, predictive analytics and the most extensive benchmark dataset on the market.
entries.verdict_short: The market leader for its powerful people science, analytics, and extensive benchmark data.
entries.praise: Its turnover prediction model can identify at-risk employee segments with over 80% accuracy, allowing for proactive intervention.
entries.criticism: The platform's power comes with complexity; it can be overwhelming for smaller HR teams or managers without data analysis experience.
entries.criticism_short: Can be complex for smaller HR teams.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-13.

entries.name: Lattice
entries.best_for: Companies looking to tightly integrate engagement data with performance management, goals (OKRs), and career development.
entries.verdict: Lattice ranks second for its excellent integration of engagement surveys with a full performance management suite, making it easy to see how goals and feedback impact sentiment.
entries.verdict_short: Seamlessly connects engagement surveys with performance reviews, goals, and 1-on-1s.
entries.praise: The ability to link a low engagement score directly to a team's OKR progress or recent performance reviews in one platform is a major workflow advantage.
entries.praise_short: Links engagement data directly to performance.
entries.criticism: Its engagement analytics and benchmarking are less mature than Culture Amp's, offering fewer filters and a smaller comparison dataset.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-13.

entries.name: Glint
entries.best_for: Large enterprises that need a scalable, robust platform for managing complex, global engagement programs.
entries.verdict: Glint, now part of LinkedIn, is the best choice for large enterprises due to its ability to handle immense scale, complex organizational hierarchies, and its AI-driven narrative intelligence.
entries.verdict_short: An enterprise-grade platform built for scale, with powerful AI for analyzing open-text feedback.
entries.praise: Its natural language processing for open-ended comments is top-tier, capable of identifying themes across tens of thousands of responses instantly.
entries.criticism: The platform can feel slow and less intuitive than more modern rivals, and its integration into the wider Microsoft/LinkedIn ecosystem is still a work in progress.
entries.criticism_short: User interface feels dated compared to rivals.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-13.

entries.name: Qualtrics EmployeeXM
entries.best_for: Organizations already invested in the Qualtrics ecosystem that want to connect employee data with customer and brand experience data.
entries.verdict: Qualtrics EmployeeXM offers the most powerful survey engine on the market, ideal for companies wanting to link employee engagement directly to customer experience (CX) outcomes.
entries.verdict_short: The most powerful survey engine, connecting employee experience (EX) to customer experience (CX).
entries.praise: The platform's ability to correlate a frontline team's engagement score with that same team's customer satisfaction (CSAT) scores is a unique and powerful feature.
entries.praise_short: Directly links employee and customer data.
entries.criticism: It is by far the most complex and expensive tool on this list, requiring significant internal resources or professional services to manage effectively.
entries.criticism_short: Very complex and expensive; requires specialists.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-13.

entries.name: 15Five
entries.best_for: Companies focused on improving manager effectiveness through a combination of engagement data, check-ins, and coaching.
entries.verdict: 15Five excels at translating engagement survey data into concrete actions for managers, with the best suite of tools for 1-on-1s, check-ins, and coaching.
entries.verdict_short: Strongest focus on manager tools, turning engagement data into coaching and 1-on-1 agendas.
entries.praise: The 'Manager Copilot' feature provides AI-generated talking points for managers to address specific team feedback in their next 1-on-1, a highly practical tool.
entries.criticism: Its core engagement survey analytics are less advanced than the top-ranked tools, lacking sophisticated heatmaps and deep statistical analysis.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-13.

entries.name: Quantum Workplace
entries.best_for: Mid-market companies that want a reliable, feature-rich platform covering engagement, performance, and succession planning.
entries.verdict: Quantum Workplace provides a solid, all-in-one talent management platform with strong survey and analytics capabilities tailored for the needs of mid-market companies.
entries.verdict_short: A reliable, all-in-one talent platform with strong survey tools for mid-sized businesses.
entries.praise: The platform includes features like succession planning and talent reviews, which are often missing from competitors focused purely on engagement.
entries.criticism: Its user interface is less modern than competitors like Lattice or Culture Amp, which can impact user adoption rates.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-13.

entries.name: Leapsome
entries.best_for: Fast-growing tech companies, particularly in Europe, that need an integrated platform for engagement, performance, and learning.
entries.verdict: Leapsome offers a highly flexible and modular platform that combines engagement with learning and development pathways, making it a strong choice for development-focused cultures.
entries.verdict_short: A flexible, modular platform connecting engagement surveys to learning and development paths.
entries.praise: Its learning module allows managers to assign specific training courses directly based on negative feedback from an engagement survey, closing the loop effectively.
entries.praise_short: Tightly links survey feedback to training.
entries.criticism: The platform's analytics are not as deep as top competitors, and it lacks the extensive external benchmarking data of Culture Amp.
entries.criticism_short: Lacks deep analytics and external benchmarks.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-13.

entries.name: Officevibe
entries.best_for: Small to mid-sized businesses looking for a simple, user-friendly, and affordable tool to start measuring engagement.
entries.verdict: Officevibe is the easiest-to-use platform on the market, making it the perfect starting point for smaller companies new to employee engagement surveys.
entries.verdict_short: The most user-friendly and accessible platform, ideal for SMBs starting their engagement journey.
entries.praise: Its setup process takes minutes, not weeks, and the weekly automated pulse surveys have consistently high participation rates (often over 85%) due to the simple interface.
entries.praise_short: Extremely easy setup and high participation.
entries.criticism: The platform lacks the analytical depth and customizability required by larger or more complex organizations.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-13.

entries.name: Bonusly
entries.best_for: Companies that want to build a culture of recognition and use that as the primary driver of engagement.
entries.verdict: Bonusly takes a unique approach by centering engagement around a fun and highly visible peer-to-peer recognition and rewards program.
entries.verdict_short: A recognition-first platform that drives engagement through peer-to-peer rewards.
entries.praise: The platform's adoption is incredibly high because employees enjoy giving and receiving points; it integrates seamlessly into Slack, making recognition a daily habit.
entries.praise_short: Drives high adoption via its rewards system.
entries.criticism: Its formal survey and analytics capabilities are very limited compared to dedicated engagement platforms; it's more of a culture tool than an analytics tool.
entries.criticism_short: Formal survey and analytics tools are basic.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-13.

entries.name: WorkTango
entries.best_for: Mid-market companies wanting to combine surveys, recognition, and rewards in a single, modern platform.
entries.verdict: WorkTango, formed from the merger of Kazoo and WorkTango, offers a competent suite that bundles engagement surveys with a robust recognition and rewards marketplace.
entries.verdict_short: A solid mid-market option combining surveys with a large recognition and rewards marketplace.
entries.praise: The platform's global rewards catalog is extensive, offering thousands of options in over 20 countries, which is great for international teams.
entries.criticism: The platform can feel like two separate products (surveys and recognition) that are not yet fully integrated, leading to a disjointed admin experience.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-13.

entries.name: Assembly
entries.best_for: Remote-first or hybrid teams that live in Slack or Microsoft Teams and want a lightweight, workflow-native engagement tool.
entries.verdict: Assembly is a wildcard because it approaches engagement not as a separate destination, but as a set of automated workflows inside the communication tools your team already uses.
entries.verdict_short: A workflow-native tool that embeds recognition, surveys, and standups directly into Slack and Teams.
entries.praise: Its 'Core Values' feature, which lets employees tag recognition with specific company values, is a simple but effective way to reinforce culture in daily work.
entries.criticism: The platform is not a fit for companies that require deep, standalone analytics or complex survey logic; it prioritizes flow of work over deep HR analysis.
entries.criticism_short: Not suitable for deep HR analysis.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-13.
